title: acs settings.json
description: Configuration generated by /init. Scopes: ~/.acs/settings.json (user), <repo>/.acs/settings.json (project, committed), <repo>/.acs/settings.local.json (machine-specific, gitignored — holds workspace_path). Per-key merge: local -> project -> user. Unknown keys are tolerated for forward compatibility.

workspace_path.description: Absolute path of the workspace folder. Required at init time; MUST be outside the consumer repo (worktrees and parallel tickets depend on it). Machine-specific: lives in settings.local.json.

ticket_prefix.description: Per-repo prefix for ticket ids (<prefix>-<n>). Required at init time; no global default.

test_coverage_percent.description: Coverage target for the /code TDD cycle. Missing the target is a hard fail.

requirements_path.description: Living requirements doc set — the standing behavioral contract, one file per feature area, accumulated ticket by ticket by /code's documentation step; read by /create-ticket and /create-spec as the area's current behavior.

requirements_layout.description: Functional/non-functional subfolder names under requirements_path (Decision B). functional_subdir holds behavioral-requirement files (one per feature); non_functional_subdir holds quality-requirement files (one per NFR item). Absent key, or an absent sub-key, resolves to the defaults below.

adr_path.description: Where /code commits accepted decision records from each ticket's design.md (default-on so decisions outlive archived ticket partitions). Explicit null disables — designs then stay workspace-only.

quality_path.description: Location of the quality/ doc set (test strategy, coverage policy) bootstrapped and maintained by /acs:create-quality. Unset = acs does not maintain this set for this repo.

operations_path.description: Location of the operations/ doc set (release process, runbooks, observability, incident response) bootstrapped and maintained by /acs:create-operations. Unset = acs does not maintain the set for this repo.

principles_path.description: Location of the principles/ doc set (engineering principles and rationale) bootstrapped and maintained by /acs:create-principles. Unset = acs does not maintain this set for this repo.

standards_path.description: Location of the standards/ doc set (coding standards and conventions) bootstrapped and maintained by /acs:create-standards. Unset = acs does not maintain this set for this repo.

e2e.description: End-to-end test layer (unset = repo has no e2e suite). When configured: /create-spec test plans must state e2e impact, /code authors/updates e2e tests in the same changeset, and the code-verifier runs the suite (green required for a passing verdict; per_iteration=false runs it only on the final, otherwise-passing iteration since e2e is slow). /create-project scaffolds the harness for greenfield repos with a user-facing surface. DEPRECATED compatibility alias for suites.e2e — kept for backward compatibility; new configs should set suites.e2e directly. When present, normalized into suites["e2e"] at settings-load time (see acs_lib.load_settings).

command.description: Command that runs the e2e suite, e.g. 'npm run test:e2e'.

setup.description: Optional environment bring-up, e.g. 'docker compose up -d --wait'.

teardown.description: Optional environment teardown; always run after the suite, pass or fail.

per_iteration.description: true = verifier runs e2e every iteration; false = only on the final, otherwise-passing iteration.

suites.description: Named test suites /acs:test runs (single source of truth for configured test commands; generalizes settings.e2e). Each entry shares the e2e sub-schema shape. The reserved name "e2e" is auto-populated automatically from a configured settings.e2e at load time (see e2e above) — do not hand-author suites.e2e directly if e2e is also set; the two are the same normalized entry.

command.description: Command that runs the suite, e.g. 'npm run lint'.

setup.description: Optional environment bring-up, e.g. 'docker compose up -d --wait'.

teardown.description: Optional environment teardown; always run after the suite, pass or fail.

per_iteration.description: true = the verifier/scheduler runs this suite every iteration; false = only on demand.

tests.description: Unit/integration test suite for the CI tests+coverage gate scaffolded by /acs:init (.github/workflows/acs-tests.yml + .acs/ci/run-tests.py, opt-in). The command MUST run the suite and FAIL on coverage shortfall — delegate to the tool (e.g. 'pytest --cov --cov-fail-under=$ACS_COVERAGE', or a jest coverageThreshold); acs exports ACS_COVERAGE (= test_coverage_percent) into the env. Read from the committed project settings.json; the CI runner has no acs install.

command.description: Runs the suite and enforces coverage, e.g. 'pytest --cov --cov-fail-under=$ACS_COVERAGE'.

setup.description: Optional environment bring-up before the command, e.g. 'pip install -e .[test]' or 'npm ci'.

models.description: Per-role subagent model and reasoning effort. Resolution per field: overrides.<skill>.<role> -> <role> -> inherit. coordinator applies only to /ship-spawned coordinators.

tracker.description: Ticket tracking backend. Tickets are always local-first; github/jira adds two-way sync via the gh / acli CLIs (which manage their own auth — no secrets in settings).

formats.description: Formats for generated artifacts. Inline fields use {placeholder} syntax; an unknown placeholder is a validation error (pre-hooks exit 2). branch_name MUST embed {ticket_id}. {ticket_ref} is pr_title-scoped only -- it renders the tracker's native reference when the ticket is synced (#<issue-number> for github, <JIRA-KEY> for jira) and the local ticket id when unsynced; it is not valid in branch_name or commit_message. Description and section templates are referenced by built-in name (pr-default, epic-default, story-default, task-default, design-default, spec-default), repo-relative name under .acs/templates/, or absolute path.

high_stakes_paths.description: Array of glob patterns identifying high-stakes code surfaces. The create-ticket planner matches changed/owned paths against these globs to RECOMMEND stakes=high. Absent key resolves to the seed default.

enforcement.description: Repo-side convention enforcement scaffolded by /acs:init: a GitHub Actions check (.github/workflows/acs-conventions.yml) and a checker (.acs/ci/check-conventions.py) that validate branch name, PR title, PR description, labels, and commit messages against formats.* — the same conventions the acs pipeline renders from. Becomes a true merge gate only when made a required status check on a protected default branch. These keys are read by the checker; the convention keys (ticket_prefix + formats) must be committed to <repo>/.acs/settings.json for CI to see them.

checks.description: Which checks the CI workflow runs (absent key = the default shown).

commit_message.description: Lint every PR-branch commit subject against formats.commit_message. Off by default — noisy under squash-merge, where only the PR title reaches main.

require_label.description: Label the acs_label check requires on every non-exempt PR (the label /acs:create-pr applies).

exempt_label.description: A PR carrying this label skips all checks (for legitimate non-ticket PRs).

exempt_branches.description: Branch-name globs (fnmatch) that skip all checks — releases, bot PRs, etc.

pr_description_sections.description: Markdown section headings the pr_description check requires in the PR body; defaulted from the configured pr_description_template.

design_sections.description: Markdown section headings the create-design structure gate requires in design.md; defaulted from the configured formats.design_template.

spec_sections.description: Markdown section headings the create-spec structure gate requires in each spec; defaulted from the configured formats.spec_template.

release.description: Settings-driven release-cut configuration for /acs:release (Decision 5, ADR 0054). Non-secret: file paths, JSON pointers, and format strings only — auth stays via gh, mirrors the tracker NFR. Absent = /acs:release cannot run for this repo (fails fast, no marketplace-hardcoded fallback); explicit null also disables it.

version_locations.description: Files + JSON pointers holding the version string, bumped to the same value on every cut.

kind.description: Resolution strategy for this entry. Only json-pointer is supported this pass; an OPTIONAL forward-compat seam — a future PR may widen this enum (e.g. regex, line) as a strictly additive extension, never a schema rewrite.

extra_refs.description: Non-version fields bumped alongside version_locations, e.g. a plugin catalog's per-entry source.ref. Optional — a repo with no such field (e.g. a plain package.json consumer) omits this or sets [].

selector.description: A name-match predicate against an array field, not a brittle array-index pointer — catalog/array order can change without breaking the bump.

pointer.description: JSON pointer to the array to search, e.g. '/plugins'.

match.description: Key/value pairs the target array entry must match, e.g. {"name": "acs"}.

set.description: Slash-separated relative path to set on the matched entry, e.g. 'source/ref'.

value_format.description: {version}-templated value written to the selector's target, e.g. 'v{version}'.

changelog_path.description: Repo-relative path to the Keep-a-Changelog file the cut drafts/dates. Only the PATH is settings-driven — the section FORMAT stays a shared convention (R-A4), also depended on by publish_driver's own extractor.

base_branch.description: Branch the 'since last tag' boundary and the release PR target, e.g. 'main'.

release_branch_format.description: {version}-templated release branch name, e.g. 'release/v{version}'. Should match one of enforcement.exempt_branches' globs (R-A5) or the release PR will fight the conventions gate.

publish_driver.description: Documentary only — names the CI job that performs the privileged tag+publish step. release_notes.py validates but does not act on this field; the skill never tags or publishes itself regardless of its value (ADR 0052).

workflow.description: Repo-relative path to the CI workflow file, e.g. '.github/workflows/release.yml'.

trigger_paths.description: Paths whose change on base_branch fires the workflow.
